name: ripgrep
description: Blazingly fast line-oriented search tool that recursively searches directories using regex patterns, respecting gitignore rules.

name: fd
description: Simple, fast, and user-friendly alternative to the `find` command with intuitive syntax and colorized output.

name: fzf
description: General-purpose command-line fuzzy finder that can be integrated with any list-producing command for interactive filtering.

name: bat
description: A `cat` clone with syntax highlighting, line numbers, Git integration, and a pager for comfortable reading in the terminal.

name: eza
description: Modern, maintained replacement for `ls` with icons, colors, Git-aware output, and tree-view support.

name: delta
description: Syntax-highlighting pager for git and diff output, with side-by-side views, line numbers, and themes.

name: starship
description: Minimal, blazing-fast, and infinitely customizable cross-shell prompt written in Rust.

name: tmux
description: Terminal multiplexer that lets you switch between several programs in one terminal, detach and reattach sessions.

name: Neovim
description: Hyperextensible Vim-based text editor focused on extensibility and usability with a first-class Lua plugin API.

name: Helix
description: Post-modern modal text editor with built-in LSP and tree-sitter support, no plugin system required.

name: htop
description: Interactive process viewer and system monitor — a friendlier, more visual alternative to the classic `top` command.

name: btop
description: Resource monitor with a beautiful terminal UI showing CPU, memory, disk, network, and processes at a glance.

name: jq
description: Lightweight and flexible command-line JSON processor for slicing, filtering, mapping, and transforming JSON data.

name: yq
description: Portable command-line YAML, JSON, XML, CSV, and TOML processor — like jq but for YAML and more.

name: curl
description: Command-line tool for transferring data with URLs, supporting HTTP, HTTPS, FTP, and many other protocols.

name: wget
description: Non-interactive network downloader supporting HTTP, HTTPS, and FTP with recursive download and resume capabilities.

name: git
description: The world's most widely used distributed version control system for tracking source code changes.

name: GitHub CLI
description: Official GitHub command-line tool for managing PRs, issues, repositories, and GitHub Actions from the terminal.

name: lazygit
description: Simple terminal UI for git commands, making staging, branching, and rebasing visual and intuitive.

name: zoxide
description: Smarter cd command that learns your most-visited directories and lets you jump to them with minimal keystrokes.

name: nvm
description: Node Version Manager — lets you install and switch between multiple versions of Node.js on the same machine.

name: pyenv
description: Simple Python version management tool for installing and switching between multiple Python versions.

name: rbenv
description: Lightweight Ruby version manager that hooks into your shell for per-project Ruby version selection.

name: Go
description: Open source programming language by Google — statically typed, compiled, with built-in concurrency and garbage collection.

name: rustup
description: The Rust toolchain installer — manages Rust versions, the standard library, and associated tools like cargo.

name: Node.js
description: JavaScript runtime built on Chrome's V8 engine for building scalable server-side and network applications.

name: Python
description: General-purpose, interpreted programming language known for clear syntax and broad ecosystem of libraries.

name: Deno
description: Secure JavaScript/TypeScript runtime with native TypeScript support, built-in tooling, and no node_modules.

name: Bun
description: Fast all-in-one JavaScript runtime and toolkit including bundler, test runner, and npm-compatible package manager.

name: FFmpeg
description: Complete, cross-platform solution for recording, converting, streaming, and editing audio and video.

name: ImageMagick
description: Powerful command-line tool for creating, editing, composing, and converting bitmap images in over 200 formats.

name: p7zip
description: Port of 7-Zip for POSIX systems with support for 7z, ZIP, GZIP, BZIP2, TAR, RAR, and many more archive formats.

name: GnuPG
description: Complete implementation of the OpenPGP standard for encrypting and signing data and communications.

name: age
description: Simple, modern, and secure file encryption tool with small explicit keys, no config options, and Unix-style composability.

name: mkcert
description: Zero-config tool for making locally-trusted development certificates with any hostnames you like.

name: nmap
description: Network exploration tool and security scanner for discovering hosts, services, and vulnerabilities on a network.

name: HTTPie
description: User-friendly command-line HTTP client with JSON support, syntax highlighting, and intuitive syntax for REST APIs.

name: Alacritty
description: GPU-accelerated terminal emulator written in Rust, focused on simplicity and performance.

name: Kitty
description: Fast, featureful, GPU-based terminal emulator with tiling, tabs, and an extensible plugin system.

name: WezTerm
description: GPU-accelerated cross-platform terminal emulator and multiplexer written in Rust, configured with Lua.

name: iTerm2
description: macOS terminal emulator with rich features including split panes, search, autocomplete, and shell integration.

name: Firefox
description: Free and open-source web browser from Mozilla focused on privacy, security, and web standards.

name: Brave Browser
description: Chromium-based browser with built-in ad blocking, tracker protection, and optional crypto rewards.

name: Visual Studio Code
description: Lightweight but powerful source code editor by Microsoft with built-in Git, debugging, and vast extension ecosystem.

name: Neovide
description: No-nonsense Neovim GUI client with smooth cursor animations and GPU-accelerated rendering.

name: Rectangle
description: Window management app for macOS using keyboard shortcuts to snap, resize, and organize application windows.

name: Raycast
description: Blazingly fast Spotlight replacement with extensible plugins, script commands, and AI integration.

name: AltTab
description: Brings Windows-style alt-tab window switcher to macOS with window previews and keyboard navigation.

name: Karabiner-Elements
description: Powerful keyboard customizer for macOS enabling complex key remappings and modifier key modifications.

name: Hammerspoon
description: Powerful macOS automation tool using a Lua scripting bridge to control the OS, windows, and system events.

name: Maccy
description: Lightweight clipboard manager for macOS that stores history of copied items and lets you search and paste them.

name: Shottr
description: Fast, lightweight screenshot tool for macOS with scrolling capture, annotation, OCR, and measurement tools.

name: CleanShot X
description: Professional screenshot and screen recording app with a rich annotation toolkit, cloud upload, and scrolling capture.

name: Obsidian
description: Powerful knowledge base and note-taking app that works on local Markdown files with a rich plugin ecosystem.

name: Notion
description: All-in-one workspace combining notes, databases, kanban boards, wikis, and project management in one app.

name: 1Password
description: Industry-leading password manager for storing, managing, and auto-filling passwords, cards, and secure notes.

name: Bitwarden
description: Free and open-source password manager with end-to-end encryption, cross-platform sync, and self-hosting option.

name: VLC
description: Free and open-source cross-platform multimedia player supporting virtually all audio and video formats.

name: IINA
description: Modern macOS-native media player built on mpv with a clean interface, picture-in-picture, and Touch Bar support.

name: HandBrake
description: Open-source video transcoder for converting video files between formats with fine-grained encoding controls.

name: Signal
description: Private, end-to-end encrypted messaging app for text, voice, and video calls with minimal data collection.

name: Discord
description: Voice, video, and text communication platform popular with gaming, developer, and online communities.

name: Slack
description: Business messaging and collaboration platform with channels, threads, integrations, and file sharing.

name: Docker Desktop
description: Platform for building, sharing, and running containerized applications with a GUI dashboard and Docker Engine.

name: TablePlus
description: Modern, native database management tool supporting PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, Redis, and many more.

name: Insomnia
description: Open-source API client for designing, debugging, and testing REST, GraphQL, WebSocket, and gRPC requests.

name: Proxyman
description: Native macOS app for intercepting and debugging HTTP/HTTPS network requests with a beautiful UI.
